question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

answer: The next two weeks should focus on stabilizing and reducing reactive work.

assessment.note: She named a concrete near-term focus—stabilizing and reducing reactive work—which signals clear priorities and some forward intent; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'stabilizing and reducing reactive work' could be unpacked next session.

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?

new_queue.label: Influence bottleneck
new_queue.name: What tends to slow decisions down when you need buy-in?

history.answer_excerpt: I can ship quickly, but I do not always feel the impact is visible.
Feedback on communication landed, and I am still processing it.
I want more room to lead cross-functional discussions earlier.
The push worked, but I do not want that pace to become normal.
I am less overloaded now, but my calendar is still fragmented.
